description_long: This is a life-sized stone sculpture of a standing male figure, reminiscent of ancient Egyptian statuary. The figure is depicted with one leg forward, arms at its sides, and wears a kilt-like garment. The head is adorned with a headdress, and the back of the sculpture features a vertical support with carved hieroglyphic-like symbols. The surface is weathered, with visible chips and erosion, particularly on the shoulders and arms. The sculpture stands on a rectangular base, which appears to be part of the same stone block. The overall color is a dark gray, with subtle variations due to aging and material texture.
description: A weathered, life-sized stone sculpture of a standing male figure with a headdress and hieroglyphic-like carvings on the back support, set on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the figure's profile, forward-stepping leg, and the vertical back support with clear depth.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the face, headdress, forward stride, and visible damage to the shoulders and arms.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, showing the other arm and leg, with the back support visible but less detailed than the front.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the full length of the vertical back support with detailed carvings and the back of the head and body.
attribute_subject_matter: standing human figure in ancient Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture is isolated on a neutral background.
